Ad Text:
Blackbook.ai is one of Australia's most successful and progressive automation companies. Our use of Data and AI to deliver on our client's pursuit of bottom-line benefits has resulted in a company that can claim a 100% success rate in solution delivery. We have an agile approach to tackling problems. We understand that there isn't just one solution to every problem! Our unique approach to problem-solving will see you working directly with our customers, capturing additional requirements and developing intuitive solutions while drawing on the vast knowledge of existing team members. 

The Data Engineer role will see you working with multiple clients, projects and stakeholders. You will find yourself working with fellow experts in the DataOps, Digital, and AI teams. You will directly report to the Head of Data and AI/ML and work autonomously to design data architectures for our clients. 

Your responsibilities as a Data Engineer are as follows: 

Work directly with clients as a Blackbook.ai consultant 

Gather requirements, design, build and test analytics solutions 

Design and build cloud platform architectures to support scalable analytics on Azure and AWS 

Design and build metadata-driven data ingestion into data lakes and databases 

Design and build data models to support highly scalable and cost-efficient data analysis 

Work with traditional BI and Modern Data Warehouse architectures 

Take ownership of the end-to-end process from requirements gathering to implementation 

Leverage the skill and knowledge of your peers, technical leads and management 

Apply best practices and be self-motivating to keep up with emerging technologies 




To be considered for the position you must have a bachelor's degree or higher, and be comfortable demonstrating your skills in the following areas: 

Professional presentation and communication skills: written and verbal, formal documentation, diagramming and modelling 

Critical thinking and problem solving skills 

Azure or AWS certification in Data Engineering or Architecture 

Designing and building relational, star and snowflake models and schemas 

Designing and building Modern Data Warehouse architectures 

Fast, high-quality development in SQL Server or a similar relational database engine such as Oracle or PostgreSQL 

Visualising data effectively using Power BI, Tableau or QlikSense 




Highly regarded although not mandatory experience includes: 

Azure Data Factory or Amazon Glue 

Snowflake, Synapse or Databricks 

PowerShell, Azure/AWS CLI, or TerraForm for managing cloud infrastructure 

In return, we can offer you: 

Competitive Remuneration 

Flexible Working Conditions 

Gym and PT 

Free Spotify Account 

Extra leave 




Blackbook.ai can offer a fast-paced and collaborative delivery environment with an emphasis on culture and teamwork, ensuring all projects are delivered with our high standards. We have a large team of dedicated consultants able to work alongside and guide you through your journey, and offer on-the-job training and development with access to multiple certifications.

Job Title: Expression of Interest - AI Solutions Developer (Applied AI / LLMs)
Ad Text:
Melbourne preferred | Australia-based | Future opportunity




About FineSkyAi

FineSkyAi is building sovereign, onshore AI capability in Australia.

We focus on applying AI to real-world problems today, while building toward a larger AI platform over time.

This is an expression of interest, not a currently funded live vacancy.




About the role

This is a hands-on role focused on building, deploying, and improving real AI solutions.

You will work directly with the founder to turn ideas and client needs into working systems across multiple domains.

This is not a pure research role. It is focused on applied AI, delivery, and execution.



Key responsibilities

- Build and deploy AI-powered applications and systems
- Integrate large language models (LLMs) into real-world workflows
- Develop APIs, tools, and automation pipelines
- Work on fine-tuning and adapting models to specific use cases
- Collaborate on solution design based on client needs
- Improve system performance, reliability, and usability
- Support transition from custom solutions to scalable platform components



You may be a strong fit if you:

- Can build and ship working AI solutions end-to-end
- Are comfortable working across backend, APIs, and AI models
- Prefer practical problem-solving over theoretical research
- Can operate in an early-stage, fast-moving environment
- Take ownership and deliver outcomes, not just code



Experience that would be useful:

- Python and modern AI/ML frameworks (e.g. PyTorch, TensorFlow)
- Experience with LLMs, prompt engineering, or RAG systems
- API development and system integration
- Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
- Working with real datasets and production systems



What this role could become

- Lead AI Engineer or Technical Lead
- Ownership of platform architecture and model systems
- Key contributor to FineSkyAi’s foundation model development



How to apply

Please send:
- your CV or GitHub profile
- examples of AI projects or systems you have built
- a short note on the most complex system you’ve shipped

AML Data Scientists (Multiple Levels) | Financial Crime Change & Remediation Programme

A leading financial services organisation is building out a high-performing team of AML Data Scientists to support a large-scale financial crime change and remediation programme.
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of financial crime prevention, leveraging advanced analytics and AI to enhance surveillance capabilities across AML, sanctions, and customer due diligence.

 What you’ll be doing
Analyse financial crime trends and identify emerging risks across large, complex datasets
Develop and deploy machine learning and predictive models to enhance detection capabilities
Apply advanced analytics, NLP, and GenAI techniques to improve transaction monitoring and surveillance outcomes
Support ongoing remediation and uplift initiatives, driving improvements across systems and controls
Collaborate with risk, compliance, and technology teams to deliver scalable, data-driven solutions
What we’re looking for
Experience in data science / machine learning roles within financial services or regulated environments
Strong programming skills in Python and SQL
Exposure to financial crime / AML / transaction monitoring / KYC / sanctions (highly regarded)
Experience with NLP, LLMs, GenAI, or advanced analytics techniques
Strong problem-solving mindset with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights
Nice to have:

Experience with SAS, Power BI, or similar tools
Exposure to deep learning, reinforcement learning, or AI model deployment
Familiarity with tools such as OpenAI, Microsoft Cognitive Services (e.g. speech-to-text, text mining, image recognition)

About the Company

Our client is a large, customer‑focused organisation operating at national scale and undergoing significant transformation.

About the Role

Our client is establishing a greenfield AI capability and is seeking an AI Transformation Lead to operate at the intersection of business transformation, AI solution delivery, and change management.

This role is not hands‑on engineering. Instead, you will work closely with business units, subject matter experts and engineering teams to identify AI opportunities, redesign workflows for human + AI collaboration, and ensure solutions are successfully adopted and optimised post‑launch.

The focus is on embedding AI into real operational and customer environments at scale, delivering measurable outcomes rather than proofs of concept.

Key Responsibilities:


Identify and shape high‑value AI use cases across operations, internal productivity and customer experiences.
Translate business problems into clear AI solution definitions and delivery plans.
Redesign end‑to‑end workflows to embed human‑AI collaboration.
Partner closely with AI engineering teams to guide solution delivery.
Lead adoption, training and change activities to embed AI into BAU.
Establish feedback loops and drive continuous optimisation post‑launch.
Track benefits and outcomes including efficiency, cost and experience improvements.
Engage confidently with senior stakeholders across business and technology.

About You


Significant prior experience delivering digital, AI or technology‑enabled transformation in complex organisations.
Proven experience taking AI or automation initiatives from concept through to production and adoption.
Strong ability to translate business challenges into structured AI use cases.
Experience redesigning workflows and embedding new ways of working.
Solid conceptual understanding of modern AI (e.g. LLMs, RAG, agentic workflows, prompting).
Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and delivery skills.
Backgrounds from consulting, transformation, product or innovation roles are well suited.
Technical/development background (Advantageous)

The Benefits


Permanent opportunity to shape a greenfield AI function
High‑visibility role with strong executive backing
Hybrid working arrangement
Competitive salary package
Long‑term career growth as the AI capability scales

Simulation Modellers - (Dynamic Simulation)

(Brisbane Based)

Do you enjoy tackling complex problems? Are you energized by digging into data and applying analytical methods to inform strategic business decisions? Then we want to talk to you!

Theia Simulation is a leader in analytics, simulation and optimisation. Founded in 2023, our team blends the expertise of The Simulation Group (est. 1999) and Boston Consulting Group. Together, we’ve supported decisions in some of the world’s most iconic operations across sectors such as mining, transport, logistics and health care. With over 800 successful projects, and a prestigious client base, we are on an exciting growth trajectory, fuelled by a rapidly expanding pipeline of new business. This growth paves the way for exceptional people to join our team. 

Why Theia? 

Our people and culture are at the heart of everything we do.  We offer a flexible work environment that promotes a sustainable work-life balance and we’ve built a culture that supports entrepreneurship, creativity and continuous learning. 

Theia Simulation isn’t your traditional consulting business. You’ll join a supportive and experienced team of consulting professionals driven by intellectual curiosity and a passion for solving complex problems.    

At Theia Simulation we pride ourselves on deeply understanding our clients’ business operations through our rigorous methodology, extensive project experience and a well-developed engagement model. We ensure clients can make business decisions with confidence.  

The opportunity:  

Dynamic simulation modelling: Use dynamic simulation to support decision making in the resources, logistics, transportation, energy and health sectors 

Project-based environment: A collaborative culture focused on delivering value that exceed our clients’ expectations 

Complex problem solving: Gain exposure to some of the most complex problems in critical industries 

Professional growth: Multiple pathways to suit individual aspirations and skillsets, providing the option to remain in a technical role or explore management and business development 

Competitive remuneration: Attractive compensation package including bonuses 

Flexibility: Full-time or part-time positions and flexible working conditions to support your work-life balance 

About you:  

Passionate problem solver: You have experience using analytical techniques to solve real-world problems  

Communication: You possess excellent written and verbal communication skills 

Programming skills: Proficiency in Python, Java and/or R is highly desirable 

Simulation packages: Experience with simulation modelling packages (discrete event or agent based), particularly AnyLogic, will be looked upon favourably 

Educational background: Tertiary qualifications in Engineering, Mathematics/Statistics, Science, Computer Science or Operations Research. Excellent academic results and postgraduate qualifications will be looked upon favourably 

Optimisation techniques: Bonus points for experience with optimisation techniques 

For a confidential enquiry please contact Gill Benfield at gill.benfield@theia.com.au

No agencies please.

Job Title: Databricks Engineer (Mid-Level) | 6 Month Contract | $700 per day | Melbourne
Ad Text:
We’re currently working with a boutique consultancy that is looking to bring on a mid level Databricks Engineer for an initial 6-month contract, with a chance of extension. This is an urgent requirement, so we’re looking for candidates who can hit the ground running.

Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines using Databricks
Work with large-scale datasets across cloud environments
Collaborate with data engineers, analysts, and stakeholders
Support ETL/ELT processes and data transformation workflows
Ensure data quality, performance, and reliability
What We’re Looking For
Commercial experience working with Databricks
Strong SQL and Python skills
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Solid understanding of data engineering best practices
Nice to Have
Experience in consulting or client-facing environments

If you’re a Databricks Engineer looking for your next contract opportunity and available to start soon, we’d love to hear from you.

Reapit – Who are we?

Reapit is the original, end-to-end business technology provider for estate agencies of all sizes. We’ve been helping sales and lettings agents to build relationships and grow their businesses for more than 25 years. Our technology connects property professionals in Europe, the Middle East, Australia, and New Zealand with buyers, sellers, tenants and landlords to power the relationships that change lives.


In Australia, Reapit stands as the preferred technology choice among the nation's leading estate agents and agencies. Tailored to the unique demands of the Australian property market, Reapit provides successful leaders with unparalleled tools across sales, property management, client relations, and data analytics, reinforcing their position at the pinnacle of real estate excellence.

What you’ll be doing

Reporting to the Global Head of Data you’ll be involved in the following;

Agentic Platform Development

Design and build scalable AI agent architecture to support autonomous task execution and workflow automation

Develop self-learning systems that adapt and improve performance based on customer transaction patterns and user interactions

Implement robust patterns and practices for multi-agent and AI augmented RPA workflows

Ensure security with customer data isolation and regulatory compliance

AI-Powered Automation & Insights

Create intelligent systems for customer data analysis, market trend identification, and anomaly detection

Build automated workflows for lead qualification, maintenance management and many other agency tasks

Platform Engineering & Integration

Architect and maintain production-ready AI services using TypeScript/JavaScript, .NET, and/or Python on AWS infrastructure & services

Integrate AI capabilities with existing Reapit systems and third-party APIs while ensuring data consistency, performance and cost efficiency.

Implement monitoring, logging, and observability solutions for AI model performance and agent behaviour tracking

Technical Leadership & Innovation

Evaluate and integrate cutting-edge AI platforms, frameworks, and tooling to maintain competitive advantage

Collaborate with product and engineering teams to translate business requirements into technical AI solutions

Mentor wider engineers and establish best practices for AI development, testing, and deployment

Who we're looking for

At Reapit, we prioritise hiring individuals who share our values and possess the right attitudes and behaviours for success. Whilst some of the listed requirements may be important, don’t worry if you don’t meet all of them, we’d still like to hear from you.

5+ years of software engineering experience with strong proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript, .NET, and/or Python

3+ years of hands-on experience building and deploying AI/ML systems for enterprise-grade solutions, preferably in a SaaS or platform environment.

Deep expertise with major AI platforms and frameworks (OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, TensorFlow, PyTorch)

Proven experience with AWS services including Lambda, ECS/EKS, S3, CloudFormation

Strong database skills with MySQL and experience with data pipeline development

Experience building autonomous agents, multi-agent systems, or intelligent workflow automation, as well as traditional RPA

Solid understanding of software architecture patterns, API design, and system integration principles

Bachelor's degree in computer science, AI/ML, or related technical field

Relevant AWS Certification in core or speciality paths

Knowledge of natural language processing, computer vision, or recommendation systems

Familiarity with MLOps practices, evaluation, and A/B testing frameworks

Experience with microservices architecture and event-driven systems

Understanding of data privacy regulations and security best practices for AI systems

Contributions to open-source projects in relevant domains

What your impact and success looks like

We expect your success and impact in the early stages of your career with us to look something like this:

Within 1 month:

You’ll be discovering how to, and actively integrate AI functionality into simple features in our products

Participating with the wider team, determining AI strategy and tooling

Within 3 months:

You will feel comfortable with the basics of the domain and Reapit’s products & services.

You will be able to demonstrate an understanding of the core areas of the technology stack.

You will be independently contributing new ideas to product and processes.

You will be contributing more advanced agentic features to Reapit products, and helping hone AI strategy from initial feature feedback

Within 6 months:

You will be able to work independently due to your continually growing understanding of our products and solutions.

Your contributions will be helping to shape the backlog and future direction of the wider products, where AI exists at its core.

You’ll be an advocate for the AI strategy throughout the business, guiding the wider teams adoption

We operate a Flexible Working Policy and we would like for you to work from our Brisbane or Sydney office, occasionally making an in-person appearance but otherwise able to work-from-home.

Don't tick all the boxes? Neither do we

We care about our industry and want it to become a more inclusive and diverse place to work. So, we’re driven by hiring not only by experience and relevance for the role but by sharing our values and the right attitudes and behaviours for success. We are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity through attracting and retaining a complementary team of employees and building an inclusive environment for all. We feel we have an empowering environment where everyone is supported and respected, and we want you to feel this too. We welcome new ideas, thinking and approaches, whilst listening to all
our employees.

“We are a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.”

We’re looking for a hands-on AI Solutions Engineer who can turn complex content, compliance, and workflow challenges into practical, AI-enabled products.

 

This role sits at the intersection of integration engineering, intelligent automation, product development, and delivery quality. You’ll design and deliver solutions that transform how regulatory, standards, legal, and risk information is collected, structured, analysed, and turned into customer value.

 

You’ll work across AI services, workflow orchestration, search, and data pipelines, helping scale our standards and compliance products while supporting expansion into GRC and risk management solutions.

This is an applied role suited to someone who can move confidently between business requirements, system design, experimentation, implementation, and QA.

 

What You’ll Be Doing:

AI Product Delivery

Design, build, and enhance AI-powered product features that solve real-world problems

Translate ambiguous business needs into clear technical solutions and delivery plans

Integrate LLMs, search, OCR, and workflow systems into production-ready applications

Define guardrails, fallback paths, and review loops to ensure outputs are accurate and auditable

Integration & Workflow Automation

Build integrations across internal systems, external data sources, and content feeds

Develop automation for ingestion, enrichment, review, and publishing workflows

Handle semi-structured and unstructured data, including low-quality source material

Improve the flow of information across products and operational processes

Compliance, Standards & GRC Solutions

Deliver AI-assisted capabilities such as document comparison, obligation extraction, and knowledge retrieval

Contribute to solutions in vendor risk, self-assessments, policy generation, and horizon scanning

Ensure outputs are explainable, commercially relevant, and aligned to customer needs

Business Analysis & Quality

Gather requirements and convert them into user stories, workflows, and acceptance criteria

Identify edge cases, risks, and validation requirements early

Partner with QA to define testing approaches for AI-driven systems

Use feedback and production insights to continuously improve performance and trust

Delivery & Collaboration

Work closely with product, engineering, QA, and business teams

Balance rapid experimentation with scalable, maintainable delivery

Communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Contribute to a culture of pragmatism, accountability, and continuous improvement

 

About You:

Essential Experience

Proven experience delivering AI-enabled solutions, integrations, or workflow automation in production

Strong experience with APIs, JSON, and system integration patterns

Hands-on experience with LLMs, prompt design, and evaluation

Experience with search, retrieval, text processing, or knowledge-based systems

Ability to translate business requirements into technical designs and delivery outcomes

Exposure to QA, testing, or validation for complex systems

Highly Regarded

Experience in compliance, legal, risk, or regulated industries

Familiarity with OCR, document parsing, metadata extraction, or entity recognition

Knowledge of SQL and at least one language (Python, C#, JavaScript/TypeScript)

Experience with RAG, vector search, or explainable AI approaches

Experience with data pipelines, ingestion workflows, and monitoring

Background working in Agile, cross-functional teams

 

Key Skills & Attributes:

Strong solution design and systems thinking

Practical, real-world application of AI

Solid integration and workflow design capability

Clear business analysis and requirement definition skills

Focus on quality, validation, and reliability

Strong collaboration and communication skills




Why Join Intertek Inform?

Work on cutting-edge AI applications in compliance and risk

Build real, production-grade AI products (not just prototypes)

Be part of a growing global organisation with strong market presence

Collaborate across product, engineering and commercial teams

Opportunity to shape the future of AI in regulatory and standards environments

Job Title: Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the Company 

SA Water is a successful, modern water utility wholly owned by the Government of South Australia. We employ more than 1,700 people across a broad range of disciplines who operate more than $14 billion worth of infrastructure to deliver essential water and sewerage services to more than 1.8 million South Australians.

We operate in a geographically and climatically diverse service area, with sites and locations from Ceduna through to Port Augusta, the Anangu Pitjantjatjara Yankunytjatjara (APY) Lands, across to the Riverland, down to Mount Gambier, and most places in between.

Our strength and expertise have been shaped by our history. For more than 165 years, South Australians have depended on us to innovate and deliver efficient and sustainable water services. With nationally and internationally recognised industry expertise, we’re focused on understanding and meeting our customers’ expectations.

As well as safely delivering smart and reliable services for our customers of today, we innovate to shape the water solutions of the future that will support thriving economies and healthy communities.

We are driven by our values; we value our people, we do work that matters, we keep it real, we know that our work has impact and we are future focussed. Staying true to our values ensures that we consistently deliver positive outcomes for all South Australians.

About the job  

The Growth Intelligence - Analytics and AI Lead plays a pivotal role in shaping how data insights and AI are used to drive smarter decision-making across Growth. Leading a small team of data experts and contractors, this role will work as a Data Product Owner and is responsible for delivering high-quality analytics, building strong data foundations and enabling scalable, AI driven solutions.  

Acting as the key interface between our transformation partner and internal teams, you’ll ensure initiatives are grounded in our data environment and aligned to business priorities.  

This is a hands-on leadership role focused on simplifying complexity, automating processes, accelerating data product delivery and embedding efficient repeatable ways of working that create real impact.  

This is a max-term opportunity until June 2027. 

About the Person

You’re a pragmatic and curious leader who understands how data and analytics come together in practice. With a strong background in analytics, reporting, data engineering, you’re comfortable navigating complex environments and translating between technical teams and business stakeholders.  

You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you have genuine interest in its potential and how it can be applied to solve real problems. You led from the front, bringing others with you, and are driven by improving how things work, whether that through automation, improve processes or deliver continuous value through smarter use of insights.  

You’re equally comfortable setting direction and rolling up your sleeves to make it happen.  

About Growth

The Growth group is at the heart of shaping South Australia’s future. This team plays a key role in supporting the state’s housing and infrastructure growth by delivering trusted water services that unlock land for development, create thriving new communities, and sustain existing regions. With a strong focus on single-point accountability, they ensure efficiency, consistent decision-making, and seamless connections for our customers. By bringing expertise and innovation together, they help drive smarter solutions and stronger partnerships that support South Australia’s growth for generations to come.

In addition, your responsibilities will include: 

Fostering trust with our people, customers and community by providing expert advice and service that ensure we effectively communicate and engage audiences with our business strategy and its delivery across South Australia, every day. 

You will: 

Lead, mentor, coach, guide the team to deliver data driven insights faster. 
Ensure the intelligence analysts, contractors and other personnel within the team achieve business objectives and goals within agreed timeframes. 
Provide timely guidance, feedback, coaching and training to help team members strengthen specific knowledge/skill areas needed to accomplish a task or solve a problem.
Lead initiatives to improve the maturity of domain data modelling, Growth Intelligence and Transformation.  
Review and determine suitability of new, and emerging AI technology, solutions/algorithms/models and recommend to be adopted by the Growth Group. 
Collaborate with teams across Growth, Planning, Systems, Customer and Technology to support integrated planning and intelligence data product delivery. 

Your success in this role will come from: 

Tertiary qualification in Data, AI, Information Technology, Business, or a related discipline.
Demonstrated experience in supporting the implementation of data solutions that align with business objectives. 
Effective communication and people skills including an ability to collaborate to deliver improved business outcomes.
Intermediate skill level in Microsoft Azure, Collibra, Microsoft SQL, data integration, data modelling, and data architecture.  
Ability to lead the team manage multiple tasks and deadlines within a continuously improving organization. 

To apply, click 'Apply'. Please submit and complete the application form, upload a cover letter and current resume. For enquiries please contact Steph Haines from the Talent Acquisition team steph.haines@sawater.com.au

Further information can be found in the attached PD - Growth Intelligence - Analytics & AI Lead

Applications close 11:55pm, Friday 1 April 2026.

A great customer experience is best achieved by having a diverse and inclusive team that reflects the community we serve. Applications are encouraged from everyone, including people living with disability, job seekers of all ages, members of the LGBTIQA+ community, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds. 

Our Industry is like no other. Dive into the wonderful world of water and explore the boundless opportunities. Learn more about the water industry at careersinwater.com.au 

Please note: We request that recruitment agencies do not submit candidates for this position, as they will not be accepted.

A lot of data science roles talk about impact.
This one measures it.

You’ll work on AI and GenAI features that sit directly in front of customers. Not internal dashboards. Not experiments that never ship. Real product functionality, where success is defined by whether it improves the experience and holds up in the real world.

This team is embedding ML and LLM capabilities into products used by thousands of customers. That means real constraints, real trade offs, and real accountability. You’ll operate in domains where correctness, trust, and compliance matter just as much as innovation.

They’ve moved past asking “can we build this?”
Now it’s “should we build this, and how do we prove it works?”

What you’ll be doing 

Frame hypotheses with product
Design experiments beyond launch
Prototype and ship GenAI/ML solutions
Take ideas from MVP to production
Apply NLP, retrieval, forecasting and recommendations
Define and track meaningful success metrics
Run offline and live experiments
Ship with ML engineers and frontend teams
Work in regulated, risk aware environments
Influence data science practices across the team
Who this suits
You’ve shipped customer facing ML or GenAI
You have hands on LLM experience
You think in hypotheses, not just outputs
You’re strong in Python and modern ML tooling
You can work effectively with engineers
You influence across product and stakeholders
You communicate clearly in complex environments
A PhD is welcome but not required. Demonstrated impact matters more.

What’s in it for you
Real products, real users, real impact
Strong ML and product peers
Influence how experimentation is done
Melbourne based, in person collaboration preferred
If you’re looking for a role where GenAI is actually in production, experimentation drives decisions, and your work reaches customers, this is worth a conversation.

nat@justdigitalpeople.com.au

Why work at Aurizn?

We are an Australian owned Defence Technology company pushing the limits to support Australian and allied forces deliver a more secure future.

Shape the world at Aurizn to make a real impact and join our team. A recognised Great Place to Work, Aurizn people are driven by our values; Think Clever, Always Deliver, Embrace the Unknown and Be Better Together.

With an industry-leading suite of benefits including ongoing training and growth opportunities, a variety of leave types, health and wellness offerings, flexible working and more, a career with Aurizn empowers you to perform at your best and push the limits for a more secure future.

Shape the world at Aurizn.

About the Role:

Aurizn are seeking a motivated Research Engineer to join a collaborative team working on advanced sensing and signal processing technologies.

In this role, you’ll contribute to the design, development, and evaluation of cutting-edge algorithms, supporting everything from concept development through to real-world testing and system integration. You’ll work closely with experienced engineers across research and software teams, helping bring innovative ideas into practical application.

Key Responsibilities:

Contribute to the development and analysis of advanced signal processing algorithms for detection, tracking, and characterisation of targets.
Support the design and implementation of array processing and estimation techniques.
Build and test algorithm prototypes using tools like MATLAB.
Analyse data, generate insights, and present findings through clear visualisations and reports.
Participate in lab and field trials to validate performance using real-world data.
Collaborate with software engineers to transition prototypes into deployable solutions.

About You:

A degree in Electronic, Electrical, or related engineering discipline, with a few years of relevant experience.
Strong foundations in signal processing and analytical problem-solving.
Experience using MATLAB (or similar tools) for modelling, simulation, or algorithm development.
A collaborative mindset and the ability to communicate technical ideas clearly.
Exposure to programming languages such as Python or C/C++.
Familiarity with sensing systems, RF concepts, or array-based processing.

Due to a requirement to be eligible for a NV1 security clearance, Australian Citizenship is required.

We welcome applicants with diverse backgrounds and experiences. Please reach out to careers@aurizn.co if you have any particular needs that we can support you with throughout this process.

The Opportunity

Mission Data (MD) Division within the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) is responsible for providing the foundational data capabilities to support all of ASD’s functions. We do this via the provision of data platforms and services, management of ASD’s mission data, and providing complex analytic development and support, including Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities.

The Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) team in MD builds and deploys machine learning systems at scale. They work with data scientists and other engineers to bring cutting-edge machine learning projects to production.

As an EL1 ML Engineer at ASD, you can expect to:

Design, develop, and oversee the production of MLOps platforms tailored for ASD.
Implement, monitor, and resolve issues related to machine learning models in operational settings.
Create and execute MLOps pipelines for the deployment of machine learning models into production.
Work with fellow engineers to facilitate seamless integration into enterprise software while remaining informed about the latest advancements in machine learning technologies and best practices.
Articulate ideas effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Persuade and engage stakeholders to foster the successful development and integration of machine learning systems and tools.
Offer technical guidance and mentorship to junior engineers in planning, designing, documenting, prototyping, and constructing intricate machine learning systems and tools.
Lead a team of ML Engineers, and recognise opportunities for learning among team members and empower them through task delegation.
Lead the scoping, specification, and accreditation processes for machine learning engineering initiatives.
Solicit and manage technical requirements on an ongoing basis.
Collaborate with technical leadership at both Branch and Divisional levels to ensure alignment of the machine learning engineering program with architectural and technical strategies.

Further information can be found at: I’m changing my career | Australian Signals Directorate (asd.gov.au).

Job Title: L2 & L3 MSP Engineers - Modern Workplace/Azure/Security/Networking
Ad Text:
Saul Recruitment - Specialising in recruiting top MSP/Cloud talent across Oz & NZ!

Looking for a role where you can work on modern workplace and cloud tech, solve real technical challenges and progress your career?

Modern Workplace/Security/Networking!
$80k - $120k + Super + Training
Sydney CBD | Hybrid WFH | Loads of Training and Career Growth!
This is a genuine local success story. An MSP that’s growing fast, investing heavily in its people, and delivering cutting-edge solutions for some fantastic clients.

If you enjoy variety, complex environments and working closely with clients, this will be a great move.

Why This Company Stands Out:

Interesting technical work: Cloud migrations, modern workplace deployments, security solutions and complex environments.
Real career progression: Training, certifications and the chance to expand your technical depth.
Supportive leadership: Managers who understand engineering and back their team.

Collaborative culture: A team of engineers who enjoy sharing knowledge and solving problems together.
This is a business that’s continuing to grow and evolve - and they want engineers who enjoy learning, contributing and making an impact.

** What You’ll Be Doing

Providing Level 2 & 3 engineering support across client environments

(L3): Delivering complex cloud and infrastructure projects
Assisting clients with forward thinking IT Strategy!

Acting as a technical advisor to clients

** What You’ll Bring:

Proven L2 or L3 MSP experience supporting multiple client environments
Experience with cloud migrations and modern workplace deployments
Exposure to security solutions and best practices (M365 Security, identity, conditional access etc.)
(L3): Experience with solution design and technical architecture

Microsoft 365
Azure
Entra ID
Intune/Autopilot
On-prem infrastructure
Backup & Disaster Recovery
Networking & Security
Scripting/automation
Windows Virtual Desktop (WVD)
Virtual Machines (VM)
SharePoint, Teams, Power Apps, and Power Automate

** What’s In It For You?

Work with a growing MSP supporting diverse clients
Learn from experienced engineers and mentors
Access training, certifications and development opportunities
Exposure to modern technologies and cloud platforms
Join a supportive, collaborative technical team
Flexible hybrid working 
This is a real opportunity to shine - and to be recognised and rewarded for it!

Referral Bonus! Know someone who would be perfect? Refer them and receive $2000 if they’re successfully placed!

Insighture is a leading technology consultancy that drives digital transformation for businesses worldwide. With a team of over 85 expert consultants, the company delivers tailored, high-impact strategies and solutions, enabling scalable product engineering. As an AWS partner, Insighture excels in co-integrated cloud services. It has collaborated with more than 50 clients globally, guiding them through cloud adoption, DevOps transformation, enterprise modernisation, and more.

The team’s expertise spans Cloud-Native Development, Solutions Architecture, UI/UX, Quality Engineering, Data Engineering, AI/ML, and DevSecOps. These capabilities empower businesses to achieve impactful and innovative outcomes.

In 2024, Insighture achieved ISO certification and was recognised as a Great Place to Work, earning three prestigious awards: Best Workplace in Sri Lanka, Best Workplace for Technology, and Best Workplace for Young People. Insighture's technology and expertise are embedded in the work of internationally recognised care providers, global freight operations, child protection systems, and health tech platforms across Australia, the UK, and Singapore.

We are seeking an experienced ServiceNow Now Assist (AI / Generative AI) Developer to design, implement, and operationalize AI‑driven capabilities on the ServiceNow platform. The role will focus on leveraging Now Assist, Predictive Intelligence, and Agentic AI capabilities to drive automation, improve user experience, and enable intelligent workflows across ITSM and related ServiceNow modules.

The candidate will work closely with platform architects, EUC / ITSM teams, and business stakeholders to deliver production‑ready AI solutions aligned to enterprise governance and security standards.

Required Experience

8+ years of hands‑on ServiceNow development experience.

Proven experience implementing ServiceNow Now Assist (Generative AI) solutions.

Strong understanding of ServiceNow AI capabilities, including: Now Assist, Predictive Intelligence, Task Intelligence, Virtual Agent and AI workflows.

Solid experience with ServiceNow scripting (JavaScript, Business Rules, Client Scripts, UI Policies, Flow Designer).

Experience working across ITSM modules (Incident, Request, Change, Knowledge).

Understanding of AI governance, security, and role‑based access controls within ServiceNow.

Ability to independently design, deliver, and support AI solutions in enterprise environments.

Preferred Skills

Experience with Agentic AI concepts and AI orchestration on ServiceNow.

Exposure to external LLM integrations (e.g., Azure OpenAI, OpenAI APIs).

Experience with ServiceNow domain separation and large‑scale enterprise deployments.

Knowledge of DEX / EUC / Service Desk analytics use cases.

ServiceNow certifications such as CSA, CIS‑ITSM, or AI‑related credentials.

Key Responsibilities

Design, configure, and implement ServiceNow Now Assist capabilities, including out‑of‑the‑box features and custom AI skills.

Develop and enhance Generative AI use cases for incident resolution, request fulfillment, knowledge summarisation, and user assistance.

Configure and manage Now Assist roles, permissions, skills, panels, logs, and usage analytics.

Implement AI‑driven automation using Predictive Intelligence, Task Intelligence, and Agentic AI concepts.

Design and deploy AI‑enabled workflows across ITSM (Incident, Request, Change, Knowledge) and related modules.

Integrate Now Assist solutions with ServiceNow workflows, Virtual Agent, and ServiceNow AI tools.

Support correlation of AI insights with operational data (e.g., incident trends, experience signals) to drive proactive remediation.

Troubleshoot and optimise AI solutions, including performance, accuracy, and access‑related issues.

Collaborate with stakeholders to identify high‑value AI use cases and translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.

Provide documentation, knowledge transfer, and support for BAU adoption of AI capabilities.
